prompt:
Before solving this problem, analyze it:
(a) What type of problem is this?
(b) What cognitive strategy is most relevant?
(c) What will the hardest part be?
Then solve it.

Respond ONLY with JSON:
{"problem_type": "...", "strategy": "...", "hardest_part": "...", "solution": "..."}

Problem: A hospital needs to schedule 4 surgeons across 3 operating rooms over
5 days. Each surgeon can perform at most 2 surgeries per day. There are
15 scheduled surgeries with the following constraints: Surgeries S1-S5
require Surgeon A or B (specialized). Surgeries S6-S10 can be done by
any surgeon. Surgeries S11-S15 require Surgeon C or D. Surgery S3 and
S7 need the same operating room (shared specialized equipment) but cannot
happen on the same day. Surgery S12 must happen before S14 (staged
procedure). No operating room can host more than 2 surgeries per day.
Provide a valid schedule or prove it is impossible.

parsed.problem_type: Constraint Satisfaction Problem (CSP) — resource allocation with temporal, capacity, and assignment constraints
parsed.strategy: Constraint propagation first (identify hard anchors like the S3/S7 room-link and S12→S14 ordering), then greedy assignment with backtracking awareness to fill remaining slots
parsed.hardest_part: S3 and S7 must share a room but not a day — this chains room assignment across days and interacts with per-room daily capacity (max 2), creating a ripple effect on surgeon load balancing
parsed.solution:
VALID SCHEDULE FOUND:

Day 1 | Room 1: S3(SurgA), S11(SurgC) | Room 2: S1(SurgB), S6(SurgD) | Room 3: S2(SurgA), S12(SurgC)
Day 2 | Room 1: S7(SurgA), S13(SurgC) | Room 2: S4(SurgB), S8(SurgD) | Room 3: S5(SurgA), S14(SurgC)
Day 3 | Room 1: S9(SurgB), S15(SurgD) | Room 2: S10(SurgA)

VERIFICATION:
• S1–S5 → Surgeon A or B only: S1(B),S2(A),S3(A),S4(B),S5(A) ✓
• S6–S10 → any surgeon: S6(D),S7(A),S8(D),S9(B),S10(A) ✓
• S11–S15 → Surgeon C or D only: S11(C),S12(C),S13(C),S14(C),S15(D) ✓
• S3 & S7 same room, different days: both Room 1, Day 1 vs Day 2 ✓
• S12 before S14: S12=Day1, S14=Day2 ✓
• Room capacity ≤2/day: Day1[2,2,2], Day2[2,2,2], Day3[2,1] ✓
• Surgeon capacity ≤2/day:
  Day1: A=2(S3,S2), B=1(S1), C=2(S11,S12), D=1(S6) ✓
  Day2: A=2(S7,S5), B=1(S4), C=2(S13,S14), D=1(S8) ✓
  Day3: A=1(S10), B=1(S9), D=1(S15) ✓
All 15 surgeries scheduled. No constraint violated.
